exemplar.title: An experiment to study the effect of the number of syllables on immediate serial recall.

detailedExemplarData.title: An experiment to study the effect of the number of syllables on immediate serial recall.

subject.title: Psychology
subject.description:
IB Psychology is a Group 3 subject in the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP). The course examines human behavior and mental processes through scientific inquiry, exploring biological, cognitive, and sociocultural approaches to understanding behavior. Students develop critical thinking skills while investigating contemporary psychological research, research methods, and ethical considerations in psychological practice.

The course is available at Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours) and Higher Level (HL, 240 hours), with first assessment in 2027.

Assessment consists of Paper 1 (integration of concepts, content, and contexts through short-answer and extended-response questions: SL 2 hours, HL 2 hours, SL 50%, HL 40%), Paper 2 (application of psychological knowledge to research contexts: SL 1 hour, HL 2 hours, SL 25%, HL 20%), Paper 3 (HL only: research methods and data analysis, 1 hour, 20%), and Internal Assessment (research proposal development: SL 25%, HL 20%).

strengths: Aim clearly linked to theoretical implications and practical applications
Detailed and accurate description of the Working Memory Model with explicit connection to the investigation
Independent and dependent variables are fully operationalized and hypotheses are precisely stated
weaknesses: One sentence in the description of list lengths is cut off midâthought
Research question placement and conciseness of hypothesis language could be improved for clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands of the introduction meet the highestâband descriptors: a wellâexplained aim, a fully integrated theory/model connection, and operationalized variables with clear hypotheses. The minor errors noted do not detract from its overall excellence, justifying the full 6 marks.

strengths: Repeatedâmeasures design is clearly explained and justified over alternatives
Sampling technique and participant characteristics are described with some rationale
Controlled variables and materials are identified, with reasons given for their control
weaknesses: No counterbalancing to address order effects, weakening internal validity
Incomplete sentence about participant demographics interrupts clarity
Limited reflection on potential sampling biases and reliability of stimuli
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration section goes beyond simple description by explaining design choices and controls, placing it in the midâband. However, gaps in addressing order effects, an incomplete demographic description, and superficial treatment of sampling bias limit it to 3 rather than the top score of 4.

strengths: Appropriate use of descriptive statistics and Wilcoxon SignedâRanks test with correct pâvalue reporting
Findings are clearly interpreted and linked back to the hypothesis
Graph is correctly presented, addresses the hypothesis, and includes clear axis labels
weaknesses: Miscalculation of the wordâlength effect difference
Graph lacks error bars and additional statistical details (e.g., effect size)
A couple of sentences end abruptly, disrupting the flow of interpretation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Statistical and graphical methods are appropriate and largely errorâfree, with clear interpretation linked to the hypothesis. Minor calculation and reporting issues prevent a perfect 6, so a score of 5 accurately reflects performance.

strengths: Discussion of findings is linked to the Working Memory Model and original Baddeley work
Strengths and limitations are stated, explained, and directly relevant to the investigation
Proposed modifications are explicitly tied to limitations and are well justified
weaknesses: Alternative theoretical explanations (e.g., interference theory) are not addressed
Inconsistency in reporting sample demographics needs correction
Theoretical critique could more deeply engage specific phonologicalâloop mechanisms
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Evaluation demonstrates strong engagement with theory, clear explanation of strengths/limitations, and justified modifications, fitting the topâband descriptor. The omission of alternative theories and minor inconsistencies justify a high but not perfect score of 5.

strandSelections.reasoning: A bar graph compares mean recall across conditions, includes axis labels and clearly links to the hypothesis. While aesthetically basic, it is correct and error-free; thus it meets the highest-band descriptor and merits a 5.

The student states statistical outcomes, interprets them in terms of rejecting the null and explains what the difference means for PSTM. Interpretation is explicitly linked back to the hypothesis and data pattern, satisfying the 5-6 band (score 5 given for some minor wording issues).

Both IV (word length: 1 vs 5 syllables) and DV (PSTM span measured via Word-Span Task) are explicitly stated and operationalised (15-item lists, scoring criteria). Directional and null hypotheses are written using the operational definitions. This fulfils the top-band conditions.

Opportunity sampling is identified; participant characteristics (age, gender distribution, bilingual status) are given and the choice is justified in terms of convenience and relevance. Although not deeply critiqued, the rationale moves beyond listing, so an explanation level (3-4) is appropriate; a mid-band score is awarded.

The Working Memory Model is described in sufficient detail, focusing on the phonological loop and its subcomponents. The student then explicitly explains how these mechanisms underpin the predicted word-length effect and why the present replication is relevant. The theoretical account is accurate and fully integrated with the investigation, satisfying the 5-6 band requirements.

Findings are related to WMM mechanisms (trace decay, rehearsal limits) and contrasted with original Baddeley work. The student discusses but does not deeply critique competing theoretical explanations, so quality is strong but not exceptional, fitting the 3-4 band at the upper end.

The student clearly states the investigative aim: to examine the effect of word length (number of syllables) on immediate serial recall. They go on to explain relevance by linking the outcome to understanding phonological-loop limits and potential applications to teaching methods that demand high PSTM. Because the aim is not merely identified but explicitly contextualised and its practical importance articulated, the work matches the highest-band descriptor.

Multiple strengths (design, sampling) and limitations (order effects, familiar environment) are explained and directly tied to how they may have influenced results. This meets the criterion of âstated, explained and relevantâ, placing it in the top band (score 5).

Materials (two 15-word lists, paper response sheets) and numerous controlled variables (time of day, presentation rate, list length, similarity controls) are identified and reasons for control are provided. The student explains why each control matters to internal validity, fitting the 3-4 band at the upper edge.

Proposed modificationsâcounterbalancing, standardised beep cues, broader samplingâare explicitly linked to the stated limitations (order effects, timing inconsistencies, evaluation apprehension) and justification is provided. This aligns with the highest-band descriptor.

The student explains why a repeated-measures design was chosen and contrasts it with an independent-samples alternative, noting control of participant variability but acknowledging order effects. This constitutes an explanation rather than a simple description, meeting the 3-4 band.

Appropriate descriptive statistics (means, SDs) and an appropriate non-parametric inferential test (Wilcoxon Signed-Ranks for repeated measures with non-normal small sample) are used. Calculations and p-value are reported accurately. Minor formatting issues exist, but statistical application is correct, justifying a high 5-6 band score (5).

annotations.comment: The spelling âBaddelyâ is incorrect when referring to Baddeleyâs work. Correct spelling ensures accuracy and credibility.
The research question could be more explicitly stated near the start of the introduction to guide the reader clearly toward the investigationâs focus and rationale.
This sentence is cut off midâthought (âeach containing 8â), which disrupts clarity. Please complete the description of list lengths to avoid confusion.
The aim is clearly linked to both theoretical implications and practical applications, demonstrating strong contextual relevance to working memory and teaching methods.
The independent variable (word length) is operationalized by syllable count with fixed list lengths, showing clear variable definition and control.
The research hypothesis would benefit from more concise language and placement of IV and DV at the beginning for immediate clarity.
Without counterbalancing, order effects could confound results. Consider implementing counterbalancing to strengthen internal validity.
The participant description ends abruptly (âParticipants were bilingual IB year 1 students,â). Complete this sentence to specify key characteristics.
The sampling section notes convenience but lacks reflection on potential biases (e.g., selfâselection, representativeness). Add deeper justification.
Materials are well described, but the reliability of stimuli (random generator vs. established lists) isnât addressed. Validate and reference stimuli selection.
The briefing footnote is formatted incorrectly and criteria for scoring are unclear. Clarify scoring rules in the procedure or appendices.
The Word Length Effect difference is miscalculated: 8.25 â 6.00 = 2.25, not 2.00. Please correct the computation.
The bar graph lacks error bars to visually represent variability. Adding error bars (Â±1âSD) would improve interpretation of statistical reliability.
The sentence ends with âindicating that withinâsubjectâ and lacks completion. Revise for full interpretation of variability findings.
Include inferential test details (e.g., degrees of freedom, effect size) to strengthen statistical reporting and replicability.
Evaluation discusses the WMM effectively but omits alternative explanations (e.g., interference theory, articulatory suppression). Integrate these perspectives.
Link discussion more directly to specific phonologicalâloop mechanisms (trace decay vs. rehearsal speed) for deeper theoretical critique.
The strengths of the repeatedâmeasures design are well articulated, emphasizing control of individual differences and increased internal validity.
Link the proposed beep modification back to the limitation of inconsistent intervals in the procedure for clearer justification.
The claim of equal gender proportions contradicts earlier data (13 boys, 7 girls). Ensure consistency in describing sample demographics.
Consider introducing a brief practice trial to familiarize participants with timing and response format, reducing learning or anxiety effects.
References in the bibliography lack consistent APA formatting (italics, hanging indents). Standardize to improve academic rigor.
The entry âWorking Memory Model. 3. The (n.d.)â¦â is malformed and incomplete. Please correct author/title details and URL formatting.
Appendix tables should include descriptive titles and column headers to guide readers without reverting to the main text.
Footnote markup uses HTML entities (â&lt;sup>â) that may not render correctly. Use proper superscript formatting.
